text_excerpt: --> In Parliament | Dave Doogan MP Angus & Perthshire Glens top of page ​ ​ ​ Home About Surgeries In the Constituency In Parliament Contact Me SSEN News More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. My Work In Parliament Speaking in the House of Commons Dave contributes regularly to Parliamentary debates on behalf of Angus and Perthshire Glens in the House of Commons - in the Chamber, in Westminster Hall and in Committees. ​ All of his contributions can be found on his social media channels, which you can find below. You can also find these on Hansard, a record of all contributions made by Dave and other MPs. Hansard Committees Dave formerly sat on the Defence Committee, the Defence Sub-Committee, and the Committee on Arms Export Controls. ​ Until January 2022, Dave also sat on the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Committee. Dave was also part of the Animal Welfare (Kept Animals) Bill Committee, helping to shape animal welfare policy. Dave is also a member of the NATO Parliamentary Assembly. All Party Parliamentary Groups (APPGs) APPGs are informal, cross-party groups formed by MPs and Members of the House of Lords who share a common interest in a particular policy area, region or country. Dave is a member of many APPGs, including: ​ Future of aviation, air travel and aerospace Arctic and Nordic Councils (Vice-Chair) Armed Forces Beer Duty CBD Products Banning Trophy Hunting, Engineering Motor Neuron Disease Norway (Vice-Chair) Scotch Whisky - Alcohol Duty Consultation Scottish Sports Eggs, Pigs and Poultry Germany Loan Charge and Taxpayer Fairness Mongolia Shooting and Conservation Grid Networks Ireland and the Irish in Britain All Videos Play Video Play Video 02:01 Energy justice will only be delivered with Scottish independence 👀 Shameless UK Government Minister trying to blame the SNP Scottish Government for Labour’s bare faced lies and incompetence in the reserved space of energy. Energy justice will only be delivered with independence. Play Video Play Video 01:39 It cannot be right to punish the poorest in our global society Play Video Play Video 03:04 A barren future for Scotland's land courtesy of the Labour Party in London! Imagine if agriculture in Angus and Perthshire Glens was owned by Unilver, Monsanto or Nestle. Massive global corporations who would buy in all their machinery, seed, fuel, fertiliser and haulage from somewhere in England or Europe. This would force a clearance of agricultural enterprise from Scotland's communities and economy. A barren future for our land courtesy on the Labour Party London. Play Video Play Video 01:34 A sovereign Palestinian state is the only hope for lasting peace and is long overdue! Play Video Play Video 01:18 Prime Minister shows complete disregard for Scotland, recycling pathetic answer at PMQs. The Prime Minister is illiterate about Scotland, our economy and our communities. He has no plans to visit Scotland and resorted to the same petty response he gave me in December. Play Video Play Video 10:03 Dave speaks in the National Grid Pylon debate. Play Video Play Video 00:49 19.01.24 Play Video Play Video 01:31 Dave Questions the Energy Minister about Overhead Lines in Angus 16.01.24 See My Recent Contributions Early Day Motions and Written Parliamentary Questions Early Day Motions Dave tables and signs EDMs to acknowledge and celebrate achievements of local people and businesses and to bring awareness to important local and national issues. ​ You can find a full list of EDMs submitted, and signed by Dave below. EDMs Written Parliamentary Questions Written Parliamentary Questions are used in Parliament to ask short questions of UK Government Ministers . Dave regularly writes to various departments to seek clarity on behalf of constituents in relation to their cases, to ask for statistics or further information, or to hold the Government to account for their policies. ​ A list of Dave's WPQs can be found below.
